drake.description: Drake disapproving something on the left vs approving it on the right. Classic format for comparing bad vs good options, old way vs new way, or negative vs positive take.
drake.recommended_structure: Two parts: left = disapproval/bad, right = approval/good. Often uses 'X:' prefix on left.

distracted_boyfriend.description: Man looking at another woman while his girlfriend looks disapprovingly. Represents choosing something new/tempting over current/safe option, distraction, or disloyalty.
distracted_boyfriend.recommended_structure: Three parts: left = girlfriend (current), center = man (deciding), right = other woman (new option).

change_my_mind.description: Steven Crowder sitting with a 'Change My Mind' sign. Used for presenting a controversial, unpopular, or spicy opinion as a challenge.
change_my_mind.recommended_structure: One confident or provocative statement that invites disagreement.

two_buttons.description: Person sweating while having to choose between two red buttons. Represents impossible choice between two bad options or two equally tempting bad decisions.
two_buttons.recommended_structure: Label on left button + label on right button. The sweating person is in the middle.

batman_slapping_robin.description: Batman repeatedly slapping Robin. Used when someone is being harshly corrected, shut down, or told their idea is stupid.
batman_slapping_robin.recommended_structure: Usually two parts: Robin says something dumb → Batman slaps him with a harsh reply.

woman_yelling_at_cat.description: Woman screaming at a calm, unbothered cat. Represents overreacting, arguing with someone who doesn't care, or one-sided anger.
woman_yelling_at_cat.recommended_structure: Two sides: angry person vs calm/unfazed party.

futurama_fry.description: Philip J. Fry squinting suspiciously. Used for doubt, skepticism, or the classic 'Not sure if X... or Y...'.
futurama_fry.recommended_structure: One caption expressing doubt or internal conflict.

one_does_not_simply.description: Boromir saying 'One does not simply walk into Mordor'. Used when something is much harder or more complicated than it appears.
one_does_not_simply.recommended_structure: One statement about something being deceptively difficult.

success_kid.description: Baby clenching fist in victory. Represents small wins, finally succeeding after struggle, or ironically celebrating something minor.
success_kid.recommended_structure: One triumphant or ironically proud statement.

doge.description: Shiba Inu with colorful Comic Sans text. Used for very simple, broken English, or ironically profound observations.
doge.recommended_structure: Short, meme-y, slightly broken English phrases.

ancient_aliens.description: Giorgio A. Tsoukalos with wild hair. Used for wild theories, conspiracy-style explanations, or 'I'm not saying it's aliens, but...'.
ancient_aliens.recommended_structure: Conspiracy or overly complicated explanation style.

roll_safe.description: Kayode Ewumi pointing at his temple. Represents 'smart' but actually dumb life hacks or obvious solutions presented as genius.
roll_safe.recommended_structure: One 'clever' but actually stupid or obvious idea.

surprised_pikachu.description: Surprised Pikachu. Used when something predictable happens but you're still shocked (often ironically).
surprised_pikachu.recommended_structure: Expression of fake or exaggerated surprise at an obvious outcome.

mocking_spongebob.description: Spongebob mocking by repeating someone's words in a dumb voice. Used to make fun of someone's argument or statement.
mocking_spongebob.recommended_structure: Usually repeats or mocks what someone said in a childish way.

is_this_a_pigeon.description: Anime guy pointing at a butterfly asking 'Is this a pigeon?'. Represents completely misunderstanding something obvious.

stonks.description: Meme Man with rising graph saying 'Stonks'. Used for ironic bad financial decisions or stupid ideas that somehow work.
stonks.recommended_structure: Ironic or stupid decision presented as smart.

left_exit_12_off_ramp.description: Car about to miss the highway exit. Represents failing at the last moment, bad timing, or almost doing the right thing.
left_exit_12_off_ramp.recommended_structure: Failing or making a bad decision at the critical moment.

philosoraptor.description: Philosoraptor (green dinosaur). Used for deep, philosophical, or pseudo-intellectual questions and observations.
philosoraptor.recommended_structure: One thoughtful or absurdly deep question/statement.

bad_luck_brian.description: Bad Luck Brian. Used for someone who has extremely bad luck in everyday situations.
bad_luck_brian.recommended_structure: One unfortunate or comically bad outcome.

first_world_problems.description: First World Problems. Used for complaining about minor, privileged inconveniences as if they are serious problems.
first_world_problems.recommended_structure: Complaining about something trivial in a dramatic way.

scumbag_steve.description: Scumbag Steve. Used for selfish, inconsiderate, or asshole behavior presented casually.
scumbag_steve.recommended_structure: One caption about doing something shitty.

good_guy_greg.description: Good Guy Greg. Used for being unexpectedly decent, helpful, or having good etiquette in contrast to expectations.
good_guy_greg.recommended_structure: One wholesome or surprisingly good action.

y_u_no.description: Y U NO Guy. Used for frustrated questions like 'Why don't you X?' or complaining about something not happening.

matrix_morpheus.description: Morpheus from The Matrix offering the red pill. Used for presenting a choice between comfortable lie and harsh truth.
matrix_morpheus.recommended_structure: Presenting two options: comfortable illusion vs uncomfortable reality.

x_everywhere.description: X, X Everywhere. Used when something is suddenly appearing all over the place (often ironically or annoyingly).
x_everywhere.recommended_structure: Something appearing everywhere in an overwhelming way.

pepperidge_farm_remembers.description: Pepperidge Farm Remembers. Used for nostalgically or sarcastically recalling how things used to be better/different.
pepperidge_farm_remembers.recommended_structure: Nostalgic or sarcastic 'back in my day' statement.

brace_yourselves.description: Brace Yourselves, X is Coming. Used to announce that something (usually annoying or hyped) is about to happen.

ain't_nobody_got_time.description: Ain't Nobody Got Time for That. Used when something is too time-consuming or not worth the effort.
ain't_nobody_got_time.recommended_structure: Dismissing something as not worth time or effort.

overly_attached_girlfriend.description: Overly Attached Girlfriend. Used for clingy, jealous, or intense relationship behavior (often exaggerated).
